threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'sorry' when you bump into someone on the pavement. Here, it's a shrug and keep walking. Welcome to Dalston!
Had a bloke on the N136 last night arguing with the driver about the bus's 'soul'—only in Brixton, eh? Everyone else just nodding along.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full suit feeding pigeons while discussing crypto with a group of hipsters at Körnerpark.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a S-Bahn station with a DJ spinning records during peak hours. Just another Tuesday in Berlin, right?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they instinctively grab a burrito and a drink from El Farolito instead of heading to a corporate chain. It’s a habit.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Only in SF do you witness a woman in a full wedding dress trying to catch a Muni, while her friends lag behind with the cake.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the hidden plazas where locals sip vermut and laugh long into the night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll find them staring at Google Maps in the middle of La Rambla, completely oblivious to the crowd weaving around them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'corner shop' instead of 'the offie'—everyone’s giving you funny looks at the deli.
Caught a night bus home and overheard two blokes arguing about which 90s sitcom had the best opening theme. Classic Bromley banter.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a saxophonist jamming outside my window at 2 AM while someone yelled 'Berliner Luft!' It felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery, serving up döner with a side of contemporary installations.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the 14-Mission during rush hour, especially when BART's acting up. It's a game of survival on wheels.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy in the back of the 14 at 16th who was warming up a burrito on the heater. Muni's real-life survival guide.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into a bar and order tapas. Here, you need to know the secret menu for the good stuff.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the bewildered look when they first try to order a 'café con leche' and end up with a 'café solo' instead.
